intro: Converting TypeScript to Kotlin transitions an application from a web platform runtime to a modern, statically typed language targeting the JVM, Android, or Native configurations. Developers switch to Kotlin to build scalable enterprise microservices or mobile apps while maintaining a modern, concise developer experience. This change replaces structural type matching with explicit nominal types while retaining highly valued features like null safety and functional primitives.

diffs.from: Compile-time structural mechanics featuring flexible utility shapes and intersection markers, erased at build.
diffs.to: Strict static nominal typing with compile-time null safety validation and generic variance parameters.

diffs.from: C-style scripting syntax utilizing explicit variable keywords and arrow configuration scopes.
diffs.to: Concise, expression-oriented layout omitting semicolons and providing trailing closures and smart type casts.

diffs.from: Generational engine-level garbage collection optimized for web scripts running on a single heap.
diffs.to: Managed JVM heap operations leveraging production collectors optimized for long-running processes.

diffs.from: Single-threaded execution loop managing non-blocking tasks via Promise queues.
diffs.to: Sequential coroutines framework providing lightweight structured concurrency across multiple underlying threads.

diffs.from: Basic type definitions requiring open-source extensions for extended OS interaction tasks.
diffs.to: Rich collection extensions, functional processing combinators, and native Java interop capabilities.

examples.title: Data Carrying Data Classes
examples.description: Declaring structural entities with automatic equality checks.
examples.fromCode:
type User = { id: number; email: string };
const user: User = { id: 1, email: "a@b.com" };

examples.title: Safe Null Casting Blocks
examples.description: Executing code conditionally depending on the presence of values.

tips.title: Utilize Built-in Smart Casting
tips.body: Once you check a variable's type via Kotlin's `is` keyword, the compiler automatically smart-casts the reference for the remainder of that block. You don't need to write explicit, repetitive type casting operations.

tips.title: Embrace Structured Coroutines
tips.body: Replace continuous Promise chains with Kotlin's coroutine wrappers. Prefixing async operations with `suspend` allows you to write non-blocking asynchronous routines sequentially without callback nesting.

tips.title: Migrate Interfaces to Data Classes
tips.body: When translating standard TypeScript object interfaces used purely as data payloads, declare them as Kotlin `data class` structures to automatically obtain built-in structural copying methods.

tips.title: Distinguish Read-Only Variable Declarations
tips.body: Kotlin enforces immutability at the variable layer using two separate keywords. Use `val` for read-only references (similar to TypeScript's `const`) and use `var` only when you need to reassign values.
